root_text.sn46.48:1.1: “Ādiccassa, bhikkhave, udayato etaṁ pubbaṅgamaṁ etaṁ pubbanimittaṁ, yadidaṁ—aruṇuggaṁ.
root_text.sn46.48:1.2: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno sattannaṁ bojjhaṅgānaṁ uppādāya etaṁ pubbaṅgamaṁ etaṁ pubbanimittaṁ, yadidaṁ—kalyāṇamittatā.
root_text.sn46.48:1.3: Kalyāṇamittassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—satta bojjhaṅge bhāvessati, satta bojjhaṅge bahulīkarissati.
root_text.sn46.48:1.4: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kalyāṇamitto satta bojjhaṅge bhāveti, satta bojjhaṅge bahulīkaroti?
root_text.sn46.48:1.5: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn46.48:1.6: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
root_text.sn46.48:1.7: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kalyāṇamitto satta bojjhaṅge bhāveti, satta bojjhaṅge bahulīkarotī”ti.

translation_text.sn46.48:1.1: “Mendicants, the dawn is the forerunner and precursor of the sunrise.
translation_text.sn46.48:1.2: In the same way, for a mendicant good friendship is the forerunner and precursor of the arising of the seven awakening factors.
translation_text.sn46.48:1.3: A mendicant with good friends can expect to develop and cultivate the seven awakening factors.
translation_text.sn46.48:1.4: And how does a mendicant with good friends develop and cultivate the seven awakening factors?
translation_text.sn46.48:1.5: It’s when a mendicant develops the awakening factors of mindfulness, investigation of principles, energy, rapture, tranquility, immersion,
translation_text.sn46.48:1.6: and equanimity, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.
translation_text.sn46.48:1.7: That’s how a mendicant with good friends develops and cultivates the seven awakening factors.”
